title: polybotrya appendiculata var. marginata
intro: Polybotrya appendiculata var. marginata, also known as Polybotrya marginata, is a plant species belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae, specifically a type of fern that is native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Polybotrya appendiculata var. marginata include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
